6.audio_text: If it seems like friendships formed in adolescents are particularly special, that's because they are. Early childhood, adolescent and adult friendships all manifest a little differently, in part because the brain works in different ways at those stages of life. Adolescence is a unique time when peer relationships take focus. And thanks to the developing brain, there are changes in the way you value, understand, and connect to friends.

7.audio_text: Teenage friends can seem attached at the hip. Scientists describe adolescents as a social reorientation, as teenagers begin to spend as much or more time with their friends than with their parents. This drive to hang with pals may be due to changes in the brain's reward center, known as the ventral striatum. Its activation makes hanging out with others enjoyable and motivates you to spend more time with them. Neuroimaging studies show that this region is highly reactive during your teenage years, which may explain why adolescents seem to place a higher value on social interactions than children or adults.
7.frame_text: In the image, we see two women enjoying a moment of tranquility in the park as they sit together on a bench, engrossed in a book. Their relaxed postures and serene surroundings indicate that they are taking advantage of pleasant weather to bond over literature, exemplifying simple joys of life and deep connections shared through mutual interests.

8.audio_text: Teenage friendships can also feel more intimate than the friendships of your childhood. This deeper connection is possible thanks to improvements in what scientists call theory of mind. Theory of mind is the ability to understand others' emotions, thoughts, motivations, and points of view, and to realize that they may be different from your own. While it may seem intuitive, this ability hinges on the careful coordination of various brain regions, sometimes referred to as the social brain. Studies begin to develop theory of mind around 18 months or so. Before that, it's thought they believe that everyone perceives and knows exactly what they know. It was once considered to be fully developed by age 5, but scientists now know that theory of mind continues to improve and mature well into your teenage years and beyond. Likewise, regions within the social brain show increased connectivity during adolescence compared with childhood. As a result, teens can better understand their friends' perspectives, allowing for deeper connections to flourish.

10.audio_text: Your ability to connect with others somewhat depends on the coordination of actions, emotions, physiology, and thoughts. This is what psychologists call interpersonal synchrony. You first show signs of the ability to sync with others as infants, synchronizing movements and babbling with your parents. As you get older and spend more time outside the home, you increasingly show this synchrony with your peers. For example, imagine walking down the street with a friend. Often without consciously thinking, you stroll at the same pace and follow the same path. You and your best friend may not be only on the same page, but also scientifically in step. So are these same processes at play when you fall in love? Take a whirlwind tour of the brain from first spark to last year with this video, or watch this video to learn why boredom can be a crucial part of your development.

It suggests that children should be introduced to financial concepts at a young age so they can understand the importance of budgeting and money management. 3.audio_text: Today, we are going to define and explain certain words and phrases that will help you become more financially literate. Let's grab our friend, Frankie Finance, to help us along the way. Needs and Wants A need is something you must have to survive, like food, water, clothing, and shelter. A want, on the other hand, is something that would be fun to have, but you can live without.
3.frame_text: In the image, we see a scientist conducting experiments in their laboratory, with a cartoon character standing in front of a blackboard as an educational element to the scene. The presence of the cartoon character indicates that humor and fun might be used to make learning more engaging, particularly for children or young learners.

4.audio_text: You know, like a skateboard, video game, or favorite toy. Knowing the difference between needs and wants can help you be more responsible with your money. Making a budget A budget is a plan for managing your money. It is based on your estimation of income. The money you earn or receive as a gift. Plus, your expenses, meaning the money you spend.
4.frame_text: The image of the boy holding a toy car serves as an example to illustrate that our initial wants can often be childlike in nature, emphasizing that focusing on basic needs and simplicity is essential for contentment and happiness. As we grow older, it's crucial to understand the difference between 'WANT' and 'NEED' to achieve inner peace and satisfaction. This concept is represented by the cartoon of a boy with a toy car.

5.audio_text: One of the main reasons to create a budget is to help you live within your means. In other words, a budget can keep you from spending money you don't have and avoid going into debt. A debt is money you borrow from someone else that must be repaid. Saving versus borrowing As you can probably guess, saving is when you set money aside for the future and do not spend it right away. Like if you are saving up for a special toy or activity. You might have a piggy bank at home where you put money that you are saving. Or you might even have a savings account at a bank. Have you ever heard of a loan before? A loan is money you borrow from someone.
5.frame_text: The animated series continues to educate children about financial concepts by incorporating a tangible element of real-world examples. 6.audio_text: Borrowing money means that you are expected to pay it back. Most often, a loan is given for a specific reason.

7.audio_text: Quite often, you will have to pay interest on the loan. This means the bank expects you to pay the full amount back. Plus an extra charge for borrowing the money in the first place. This extra is called interest and is a percent of the borrowed amount. A savings account is a place where you put money that you intend on saving for a while. And guess what? You can actually earn a little extra money while your money is in the bank. For real! This is also called interest. But instead of giving money back to the bank for a loan, it is a little extra money that you earn while your money sits in the bank. A checking account is another kind of bank account that you can use to pay bills. Either by check or by a special card called a debit card.

9.audio_text: Credit vs. debit A credit card is a small hard plastic card that is issued by a credit card company or other financial institution. They are basically a type of loan. Because using a credit card means you are borrowing money from the company who issued the card. Here's how it works. The credit card company loans you money and you pay it back according to a schedule. This is usually one payment per month with interest on the unpaid amount. On the other hand, debit cards are linked directly to your bank account. Which means you use your debit card to pay for things with money you already have. In either your savings or checking account.

3.audio_text: In addition to this, although dolphins have eyes to see. They even have the ability to echolocate. Which can help them navigate better, especially in murky water. To echolocate, dolphins produce high-frequency sound waves. Which travel through water and get reflected from obstacles as echoes. When these echoes return to the dolphins. They are able to identify the location, shape, etc. of the obstacles. Hence, using this method, they easily avoid the obstacles and navigate in murky water. Moreover, it is also observed that using low-frequency sound. Dolphins communicate with each other. They even have signature whistles. Like names, which they use to call each other. According to a study, it is also believed that dolphins can recognize themselves in the mirror. This quality of physical self-awareness is not usually present in other animals.

0.audio_text: 选鲸鱼有六看,家庭飼养鲸鱼不仅有观赏价值,而且有一身新健康美化环境。挑选鲸鱼并非是一件轻而易举的事,得通过认真细心的观察。第一看外表,在玻璃缸内游动的鲸鱼,只要认真观察,有毛病的鱼是可以看出来的。若是游在水中指晃脑袋不动身子,证明营养不良,游动疲倦腹部骨不起来,可能患有余受病。
0.frame_text: The video captures a lively scene inside an aquarium, where a school of blue and white fish are swimming freely in the water. They display vibrant colors and are surrounded by an assortment of other fish that add to the diversity and richness of the aquatic environment.

1.audio_text: 脱离群体的鲸鱼一般不健康,色彩再好看也不是优质鲸鱼。第二看颜色,鲸鱼的颜色一般很鲜艳挑选时要注意灰色的或黑灰色的品种里,黑色的不能要。红黄的或黄色的品种里,非红色的不能要,有的鱼体成半红半黑色,或是深红而齐叶边缘黑,看起来很美,其实过一段时间就会变成红色。第三看雾区,市场上的鲸鱼品种很多,挑选不慎就会进入雾区。有的鲸鱼患有数林病,往往被误认为是珍珠林鲸鱼。在挑珍珠林鲸鱼时,要调体形两头尖,像个早合适的,头和尾柄不尖的不合要求。第四看体形,鲸鱼孵出后长成幼鱼,要经过很多环节,在搬运过程中,难免有的林条,林片撕破脱落,在捞出捞进中被林、尾林,其业异蛇一列腹部两侧的鱼林也一掉落。特别是红色和黑色的鱼掉林后,鱼体会露出表皮的灰白色,影响观赏。第五看病态,鲸鱼常见病态有,一白点并幼鱼的尾部和塞盖上有白点。二梅军病,鱼腹两侧容易擦伤部尾出现白色军丝。三白蒙病,鱼腹两侧有白霜。四师病,鱼师如同仁师一样。五树林病,鱼肚皮上有树起的林丝,两塞盖半圈发红。六车轮虫病,换这种病的鱼喜欢贴边油。

0.audio_text: Hi guys, welcome back to Drink Tutorials. Today I'm going to teach you how to make the Manhattan. Manhattan is one of the oldest cocktails. It was invented somewhere around the 1870s in Manhattan, hence the name Manhattan, and there are a lot of various stories, pretty colorful ones, about its origins and I promise you that one of these days I'll do a video that goes into the origins and then also little tips and tricks. Now when you're making Manhattan, which is a very simple drink, it's just a two-to-one ratio, sweet vermouth, rye whiskey, and angostura bitters, you need to keep in mind that whiskey selection is really important. So what I like to do for just a standard Manhattan is old overhauled rye whiskey and coque vermouth. The coque vermouth is a really high quality vermouth. You can get it in 375 milliliters as opposed to a full 750, so you're not wasting and you got to be careful because even though you can keep this in the fridge it will go bad in about three months. So the smaller bottle is really a lot better. If you decide that you want to make want like you want to really raise the palette up and you really want to get a spicier, more full-bodied Manhattan, you can do that by supplanting it with 100 proof rye. I like to use Rittenhouse, but then you got to make sure that you got to be in proportion with your vermouth selection and that would be like something like Carpano Antica. You can experiment with different vermouths and honestly we're going to do rye Manhattan's because rye Manhattan's are traditional and I'm not even going to go into the whole you know bourbon thing. If you guys want to do bourbon Manhattan's don't tell me about it and I won't slap you. Alright so to make a Manhattan you do one, two, three.

6.audio_text: And this allows us to update the model more frequently. When you do this though, the loss graphs are expected to be a lot noisier, but why? Here at Training Run, where on one of your iterations, your mini-batch grabs a lot of dogs but only a few cats. Since your batch has more dogs, and the loss is more impacted by the ability to classify dogs correctly, you update your weights to detect dogs better. But then on your next iteration, your batch has more cats. Perhaps the adjustments made to handle dogs better made it more difficult to detect cats, and your loss increases this time. Since we're working with batches, what your network learns during an iteration is based on the properties of that mini-batch, and this can lead to noisier graphs. But nonetheless, it is still learning patterns and you should still expect for the loss to trend towards a minimized error. Another benefit that's a little tricky to consider is that training on all your data at once might not even be the best choice anyways. The best way to think about this is the realization that your dataset is likely only a subset of all possibilities. Your network can only learn patterns from the data you have, and if you throw in all of it at once, you risk getting stuck in a local minimum. A minimum that's highly constrained to what you have. Whereas if you use mini-batches, you have the potential for the resulting noise to carry you out of these local minima. Second, after using an ability, Death in Lavender grants Belveth bonus attack speed for her next two basic attacks. She can hold up to six stacks at a time. Belveth's Q is Void Surge, which allows Belveth to dash and deal damage to all enemies she passes through, applying Unhit effects to the first target hit. Void Surge allows Belveth to dash in four different directions, with each direction having its own unique cooldown. Cool Down for Void Surge scales on attack speed, so make sure to take advantage of Death in Lavender to speed up the inevitable doom of all of your enemies. My word awaits. You cannot dash through walls with Void Surge while on an empty stomach, but more on that later. With her W, above and below, Belveth slams her tail to the ground, damaging, knocking up and slowing her enemies. If a champion is hit, above and below will refresh Void Surge in the direction of that champion, giving Belveth an avenue to gap close and finish her prey. Be gone! Belveth's E, Royal Maelstrom, roots her in place while she channels a storm of slashes around her that targets the lowest health enemy, minions and monsters included. But be advised that Belveth would never make herself a sitting duck. Royal Maelstrom also gives her damage reduction and life steal, while doing increased damage based on the target's missing health. 1.audio_text: Say a ball is moving on a flat ground and there's no external force acting on it. Will the ball eventually come to a halt? Yes, it will. Have you ever wondered why? Say you're skating on an ice rink and your friend is skating on the road. Whose movement do you think will be smoother? Definitely yours. When you hold a bottle in your hand, why doesn't it just slip through and fall? Sometimes when you're walking around in a mall, do you remember seeing a caution sign to warn you about the floor being wet? What explains all this? The answer is friction. The reason why the ball eventually comes to a halt is because of the force of friction. The reason why skating on an ice rink is easier because the force of friction is lesser there. The bottle doesn't slip through because the force of friction between your hand and the bottle doesn't allow it to do so. If the floor is wet, the friction offered by it reduces and there are chances that you might slip and fall. So what is this friction we are talking about? It's quite easy to understand its concept. Friction is nothing but a force. It is the force exerted by a surface where an object moves across it. In the first case, when the ball is moving in this direction, the force of friction is acting in the opposite direction. The force of friction offered by the ice rink floor is lesser than the force of friction offered by the road. Hence skating on the ice rink is easier and seems effortless. The bottle does not slip down because the surface of your hand offers a friction in the upward direction. In the case of the floor in the mall, the soapy water reduces the friction offered by the floor. In the coming lessons, we will learn a lot more about friction. We will see the factors affecting friction. We will understand if friction helps us in our everyday life or not. And we will also cover the different types of friction.

2.audio_text: Welcome to the N'Firi Champion Spotlight. It's lonely at the top of the food chain. N'Firi's passive is We Are More. N'Firi passively spawns mini-me packmates that mirror her attacks and assist her abilities. Aw, what a good girl. Hitting champions with abilities or last hitting anything reduces the cooldown of a packmate spawn. As N'Firi levels up, her spawn rate and maximum capacity will increase. Each packmate helps N'Firi last hit occasionally, has its own health, and can eat skill shots, but not your homer. Thanks to darkened corruption, N'Firi isn't just the leader of her pack, she is the ball at once. Unlike other champions who command their lowly minions or just go it solo, N'Firi and her packmates truly play as one. She's got that dog in her, and another dog, and another. And they love to make fetch happen. What a mean girl. N'Firi's Q fires darkened daggers, which damage and bleed all enemies hit, executing minions and small monsters if they're brought low enough. Packmates will swarm the first champion hit in a deadly dog pipe. Taste cracked enemy. N'Firi can recast his ability within a short window. If it hits a bleeding enemy, the remaining bleed will be dealt instantly, plus missing health damage. And if the target is a champion, N'Firi will heal. This ability gives N'Firi quite the edge in most lanes. She's got range advantage over many other assassins, and of course, everyone tastes delicious after a second dagger hit. When N'Firi's ready to roam in search of larger meals, the minion exsitudes will help her push quickly. Burst expand our bleeding grounds. Mobility is an assassin's favorite treat. With hounds per suit, N'Firi briefly channels before dashing to an enemy. Her packmates pounce after her, dealing extra damage for each puppy pal. N'Firi will chase her enemy within a wide range. If an enemy Ezreal or Leblanc is trying to slip out of trouble, she won't give up that easy. But if her target gets very far away, there's no hounds allowed. Thanks to this ability, N'Firi is the top dog when it comes to diving onto squishy targets. But be careful, other champions can block the impact of her charge. Plus, while she's channeling, her packmates go un-targetable, which leaves her open to all kinds of pesky skill shots. N'Firi's E, Eviscerate, lets her dash a short distance, damaging enemies into her path. When she lands, N'Firi explodes in a swirl of steel. Her packmates are recalled and healed to full, which is useful for leashing them out of a pinch. N'Firi can combine this dash with her W to cover a lot of ground quickly, expanding her onto grounds to unsuspecting lanes. But take caution around champions with lots of CC because they're looking to play dog catcher. Once N'Firi pops both dashes to engage, she can't escape until someone's diced up. With a mighty howl, N'Firi runs wild with her ultimate, the Call of the Pack. In a flurry of blades and forks, she spawns bonus packmates to assist her. Each packmate also gets a health and move speed boost, kind of like my dog when it's dinner time. N'Firi gains attack damage, out of combat move speed, a burst to vision, and a shield which he first attacks a champion. On her first takedown, all these effects are refreshed, making her positively lethal. Of all the buffs on N'Firi's ultimate, don't underestimate the vision boost. The better to see you with, my dear. The shortcut to creating a perfect moment for exercising your killer instincts. If you reveal an enemy in your headlights, like a fed ADC, use W to lock onto them and rush them down your full horde of hounds. It's a dog eat dog world. N'Firi was born from the concept of a beginner assassin, the ideal champion to learn the class. She's kind of like a Pantheon or Zed who wants to get ahead quickly, then chomp down across the map to grab an early win. For most assassins, the difference between a big play and a fumble is sniffing out when to pick a fight. Managing your mini-mes and fighting around them is what sets good N'Firi players apart from the pack. Seven atrocs, so driven to die. Creating the perfect conditions to go in for the kill is essential for any master assassin. After N'Firi's lessons, any pup can grow under the pick of the litter. With the full force of her vicious vessels, N'Firi reigns at the top of the food chain.

1.audio_text: 讓第一共和銀行暫時沒有爆雷但是恐慌引發的洋群效應足以壓垮任何銀行美國早前三家銀行倒閉的驚嚇顯然還在困擾著市場其實從美國歸股銀行到瑞士信貸然後再到現在的第一共和銀行金融業人士跟財經評論界一直被問到的一個問題就是這是另一次的雷曼時刻嗎?這是歐美金融業崩潰的開始還是說是另一個獨特性的個案呢?其實雷曼時刻會一直一直被提起也是因為那是一次對全球金融慘痛的經驗雷曼時刻指的是2008年9月15號雷曼兄弟宣布破產的那一刻當時受美國次貸危機的影響美國投資銀行領域四大天王之一的雷曼銀行宣布破產倒閉立馬就將美國次貸危機升級成了全球金融危機由於這個事情影響深遠所以雷曼時刻就一直被隱身用來形容一些經濟金融領域的爆雷事件當時雷曼兄弟的破產引發了全球金融市場的恐慌還有信心崩潰很多銀行還有金融機構因此而受到衝擊並且陷入困境導致金融市場流動性嚴重不足影響了全球經濟的發展英國BBC的一篇分析報導提到當年雷曼兄弟倒閉歸根舊底是一位NBS房產抵押貸款正確的信用崩盤而NBS經常作為抵押品進行借款甚至還通過延伸品進一步放大了槓桿率由於它被廣發運用在各種金融交易當中因此傳染性非常的強而相比之下無論是歸股銀行還是瑞信股價在暴跌背後都沒有出現某種特定資產的崩盤雖然很多分析都認為歸股銀行跟瑞士信貸不會造成下一個雷曼時刻但是全球對沖基金巨頭橋水創始人達利歐卻發文表示歸股銀行倒閉僅僅是短期債務週期泡沫破滅的開端他認為未來一兩年美國的經濟金融情況將會非常嚴峻那有句話是這麼說的雪崩的時候沒有一片雪花是無辜的危機總是在不經意的時候慢慢累積直到爆發的那一刻大家才恍然大悟因此最近這幾次的銀行爆雷事件到最後會不會越演越烈也還是一個未知之屬
